defect: D3 fifo-tiebreak violated: at a single price level the matcher fills the MOST-RECENTLY-rested order first (LIFO) instead of the earliest (FIFO). Differs from queue-jump in that the violation is in the MAKER (resting) ordering, not the taker arrival ordering.
must_turn_red_gate: exchange-lob differential (D2 whole-output vs FIFO reference)
how_the_gate_rejects: Two sells rest at 99: id1 (earlier) then id2 (later). An aggressor buy for 4 must consume id1's 3 first (FIFO), then 1 from id2. The corrupted fixture consumes id2 first (LIFO). The differential gate diffs makerId per trade and REJECTS: correct first maker is id1, corrupted first maker is id2.
why_per_trade_checks_miss_it: Both makers rest at the same price 99, so I1 (trade-at-resting-price) is satisfied either way; I2/I3/C1 hold. Only the FIFO-ordering of equal-price makers (D3) distinguishes correct from LIFO — a whole-sequence property invisible to per-trade validity.
